title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain Himachal Pradesh judicial officer’s plea against Collegium picks for HC judgeship
content:
• On June 22, 2026, the Supreme Court, through a Bench including Justices B.V. Nagarathna and Joymalya Bagchi, refused to entertain a petition filed by judicial officer Arvind Malhotra against the Collegium's picks for Himachal Pradesh High Court judges.
• The Supreme Court Collegium, headed by Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant, had on June 2 recommended Chirag Bhanu Singh, Bhupesh Sharma, and Yogesh Jaswal for elevation as judges of the Himachal Pradesh High Court.
• The Supreme Court observed that recommendations for appointments to constitutional courts are based on the Collegium’s subjective assessment, are amenable to only limited judicial review (e.g., for lack of effective consultation or eligibility issues), and seniority alone does not confer a right to be recommended for elevation.

title: 75,487 traffic e-challan cases settled in two days under 50% fine concession scheme
content:
• A total of 75,487 pending traffic e-challan cases were settled across Karnataka.
• ₹2.24 crore was collected through various prescribed modes of payment during the two-day period.
• The settlements were made under the State government’s recently announced 50% fine concession scheme.

title: X suffers global outage, thousands of users report problems accessing platform
content:
• Social media platform X, formerly Twitter and owned by billionaire Elon Musk, experienced a widespread global outage on Monday (June 22).
• More than 1,300 users in Canada reported issues with X as of 10:16 a.m. ET during the outage.
• In the United Kingdom, over 1,400 users had reported service disruptions by 10:18 a.m. ET due to the X outage.

title: US backs India's combat readiness with proposed $482.2 million Apache helicopters and M777A2 howitzers support deal
content:
• The United States has formally notified a proposed sale of sustainment support services for India's AH-64E Apache attack helicopters and M777A2 Ultra-Light Howitzers, valued at an estimated $482.2 million.
• The support package for the M777A2 Ultra-Light Howitzers is estimated at $230 million, and for the AH-64E Apache helicopter sustainment programme is valued at $198.2 million.
• The principal contractors for the Apache sustainment package will be Boeing and Lockheed Martin, while BAE Systems (based in Cumbria, UK) will be the principal contractor for the M777A2 support programme.

title: ‘Final deal is the house’: Vance says Switzerland talks with Iran set ‘good foundation’ to end war
content:
• US Vice President JD Vance and Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf held peace talks in Switzerland, creating a "good foundation" for a final agreement to end the war.
• Mediators Pakistan and Qatar announced encouraging progress, establishing a roadmap towards reaching a final deal within 60 days following talks held from Sunday into Monday.
• The U.S. administration may release frozen Iranian funds to allow purchases of American agricultural goods like soy, corn, and wheat, with Qatar having approval over the process.

title: Calcutta Stock Exchange may rise again as BJP govt unveils ‘revival’ plan in Bengal budget 2026
content:
• The West Bengal government, led by Finance Minister Dr. Swapan Dasgupta, proposed a plan to revive the Calcutta Stock Exchange (CSE) during the presentation of the 2026–27 state budget.
• The Calcutta Stock Exchange (CSE) has been inactive since April 2013, when it was suspended by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) for failing to meet regulatory requirements.
• In the 2026–27 West Bengal state budget, Finance Minister Dr. Swapan Dasgupta announced a ₹36,000-crore outlay for the Annapurna Yojana and a 20 percentage point hike in Dearness Allowance (DA) for government employees.

title: Andy Burnham vs Wes Streeting — who will be the next UK PM and how will he be chosen as Keir Starmer resigns? Explained
content:
• Keir Starmer resigned as the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom and leader of the Labour Party on Monday, with a new PM expected to take over by September.
• The Labour Party's leadership contest will have nominations opening on July 9 and closing by mid-July, aiming for a new leader to be in place by September if a contest occurs.
• Andy Burnham has confirmed his candidacy and could become Prime Minister around July 17 if he is unopposed in the leadership contest, before the Commons summer recess on July 16.
